summary.intent: Fix a timeout error when adding an OpenAI-compatible provider via the UI without specifying a model ID, by adding an early-return discovery path that queries /v1/models instead of probing chat completions.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented the fix: made `fetch_models_from_api` public in openai.rs, added early-return discovery path for custom providers in validate_key, added two regression tests, and all 296 tests pass with clean formatting and no new clippy warnings.

repo: Ollama already had an early-return pattern in validate_key (lines 2289-2368 of provider-setup/src/lib.rs) that custom providers lacked — the fix mirrors this pattern.
Custom providers are identified by the 'custom-' prefix via is_custom_provider().
is_chat_capable_model() from moltis_providers is used to filter discovered models before returning them to the UI, consistent with the probe-succeeded path at line 2599.
The OPENAI_MODELS_ENDPOINT_PATH constant maps to /models, not /v1/models — the base_url already includes the /v1 prefix.
Clippy failures in moltis-telegram crate are pre-existing and related to CUDA not being available on macOS; macOS-specific lint target excludes CUDA crates.
The 'beads' issue tracking system was unavailable during this session.

code.finding: fetch_models_from_api was a private async fn fetching /v1/models with 8s timeout — made public as pub async fn to allow cross-crate usage from provider-setup.

New early-return block inserted after Ollama block: if is_custom && selected_model.is_none(), calls fetch_models_from_api, filters via is_chat_capable_model, returns discovered models without probing chat completions.
The probe-succeeded path already filters models with is_chat_capable_model — the new discovery path mirrors this.

workflow: When mock server paths don't match, run the test with output to see the actual HTTP request path rather than guessing.
Running clippy on only the changed crates (providers + provider-setup) avoids noise from pre-existing failures in unrelated crates.
The nightly formatter (cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt) is required per project convention — standard stable fmt may not suffice.

friction: The OPENAI_MODELS_ENDPOINT_PATH constant resolves to /models not /v1/models, causing the initial test mock to serve the wrong path — required a debug run to discover.
Multiple Edit attempts needed uniqueness disambiguation when inserting into large lib.rs file with repeated patterns.
The beads issue tracking system was down, preventing issue creation before committing.
Clippy cannot run on the full workspace on macOS due to CUDA crates — requires a platform-specific exclusion flag.
open_items: Beads issue was not created due to DB being down — should be filed when the system is back up.
The PR was requested but the transcript ends at commit — PR creation outcome is not captured.
No changes were made to crates/web/src/assets/js/providers.js as it already handles valResult.models at line 428, but this assumption was not verified in the transcript.
